justification: All five pass criteria met: (1) Atomic acceptance identified and solved via Redis SETNX with Lua script for atomic check-and-set, (2) In-memory state recommended with explicit durability trade-off acknowledged (Redis volatility, AOF persistence, write-through after SETNX), (3) Fallback auto-assignment on timer expiry designed via sorted-set polling timeout scheduler selecting highest-rated available courier, (4) Hot key risk addressed indirectly through thundering herd analysis and Redis cluster/sentinel recommendations, (5) Trade-offs section explicitly covers durability vs latency (Redis SETNX sub-millisecond vs PostgreSQL 5-20ms, volatile memory vs AOF persistence).

observations.text: SETNX as the atomic primitive is clearly identified, thoroughly explained, and correctly positioned against alternatives (database locking, Redlock, optimistic concurrency)
Durability vs latency trade-off is explicitly called out in trade_offs[0] with concrete numbers (sub-millisecond vs 5-20ms) and mitigation (AOF, write-through)
Fallback auto-assignment is well-designed with timeout scheduler using sorted-set polling, including re-check of availability before fallback assignment
Race condition between acceptance and timeout at T=14.99s/T=15.0s is explicitly identified and resolved via SETNX semantics
Lua script recommendation for atomic check-state-and-claim eliminates check-then-act races — shows deep Redis expertise
Clock skew risk addressed elegantly by using Redis as single time authority rather than distributed clocks
Excellent separation of concerns — Gateway as thin routing layer, stateless Bid Window Manager, Redis as serialization point

Hot key risk is not explicitly named as such — the thundering herd and zone-based fanout discussion covers the symptom but doesn't directly call out Redis hot key/shard concentration for popular zones
Redis SPOF risk explicitly identified as critical severity with concrete mitigation (Sentinel, circuit breaker, degraded direct-assignment mode)
Implementation guidance is exceptionally detailed with phased rollout, specific file paths, test scenarios for race conditions, and clear pitfalls-to-avoid list
